query: Magnificent 7 tech companies (Alphabet, Amazon, Apple, Meta, Microsoft, Nvidia, Tesla) large acquisitions $10 billion or more announced or rumored in 2026, especially mid-2026. Any pending big M&A deals as of June-July 2026?
answer:
I've completed my research. Here's the summary of findings on Magnificent 7 large acquisitions ($10B+) in 2026:

## Bottom Line

**No Magnificent 7 company has a *pending* (announced-but-not-yet-closed) acquisition of $10B+ as of June–July 2026.**

### Key findings:

1. **Alphabet/Google — Wiz ($32B)** — The only completed $10B+ Mag 7 deal of 2026. Closed March 11, 2026 (announced March 2025). Largest acquisition in Google's history. Already closed, so not pending.

2. **Nvidia — PC-company rumor (DENIED)** — SemiAccurate reported (Apr 13, 2026) that Nvidia was negotiating to buy "a large PC-oriented company" (speculation pointed to Dell/HP), but **Nvidia flatly denied it as "false."**

3. **Meta — All deals below $10B** — Manus (~$2B, ordered unwound by China in April 2026), Assured Robot Intelligence (undisclosed). Its $14.3B Scale AI deal (June 2025) was a 49% *non-voting minority investment*, deliberately structured to avoid a merger review — not a $10B+ acquisition.

4. **Tesla — SpaceX merger speculation** — The biggest large-scale chatter involving a Mag 7 name. A SpaceX–Tesla combination (~$3.4T combined value) is heavily speculated (Wedbush's Ives at ~80% odds), especially after SpaceX's June 2026 IPO, but has **not been formally announced or filed**. Tesla also disclosed a small (up to $2B) AI-hardware acquisition.

5. **Apple, Amazon, Microsoft — No $10B+ deals.** Apple's notable move was a ~$1B/year Gemini-in-Siri partnership (a commercial deal, not an acquisition).

**Context:** 2026's actual megadeals are outside the Mag 7 (Netflix–WBD ~$83B, PIF/Silver Lake–EA $55B, Charter–Cox $34.5B). The hyperscalers are channeling capital into ~$650–700B of AI infrastructure capex rather than large M&A.

sources.title: Big Tech set to spend $650 billion in 2026 as AI investments soar

key_facts: Alphabet/Google's $32B acquisition of Wiz is the only completed $10B+ Magnificent 7 deal in 2026; it closed March 11, 2026 (announced March 2025) and is the largest acquisition in Google's history.
No Magnificent 7 company has a PENDING (announced-but-not-yet-closed) acquisition of $10B or more as of June–July 2026.
Nvidia was rumored (SemiAccurate, Apr 13, 2026) to be negotiating to buy a large PC-oriented company (speculation centered on Dell/HP), but Nvidia flatly DENIED the report, calling it 'false.'

Meta's largest 2026 acquisition activity is well below $10B: Manus (~$2B, late Dec 2025; ordered unwound by China's NDRC Apr 27, 2026) and Assured Robot Intelligence (undisclosed, May 2026). Its $14.3B Scale AI deal (June 2025) was a 49% non-voting minority investment, not a $10B+ acquisition.

Tesla disclosed a small (up to $2B) AI-hardware acquisition in its Q1 2026 10-Q; a much-discussed SpaceX–Tesla merger (combined value ~$3.4T) remains speculation and has NOT been formally announced as of mid-2026.
Apple, Amazon, and Microsoft have no $10B+ acquisitions in 2026; Apple's notable move was a ~$1B/year Gemini-in-Siri commercial partnership with Google (Jan 12, 2026), not an acquisition.
2026's biggest megadeals are outside the Mag 7 (e.g., Netflix–Warner Bros. Discovery ~$83B pending, PIF/Silver Lake–EA $55B, Charter–Cox $34.5B).
Big Tech hyperscalers (Alphabet, Amazon, Meta, Microsoft) are prioritizing ~$650–700B in 2026 AI infrastructure capex over large M&A.

data_points.metric: Alphabet/Google acquisition of Wiz (largest 2026 Mag 7 deal; closed)
Meta investment in Scale AI for 49% non-voting stake (2025, not a $10B+ acquisition)
Meta acquisition of Manus (ordered unwound by China Apr 2026)
Tesla disclosed acquisition of unnamed AI hardware company (up to)
Apple-Google Gemini-in-Siri commercial partnership (annual, not an acquisition)
Estimated combined valuation of speculated SpaceX-Tesla merger (unconfirmed)
Dell share price jump on Nvidia PC-acquisition rumor (later denied)
Projected 2026 AI infrastructure capex by four hyperscalers (Alphabet, Amazon, Meta, Microsoft)
